troubleshooting.title: Resolve a validation failure in a CI/CD pipeline
troubleshooting.shortdesc: When the validation step in your CI pipeline fails, your pull request is blocked. This topic shows you how to find the failure report, reproduce the failure locally, and resolve the specific issue.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Diagnose and fix a markdown-validator step that fails in a CI/CD pipeline, including how to read the failure report and reproduce the failure locally.

condition.title: Situation
condition.p: Your pull request shows a failing check in CI. The check is named something like Validate documentation or validate. The merge button is blocked.

cause.title: Cause: A Required rule failed
cause.p: One or more Required validation rules failed on at least one file in your pull request.

step.cmd: Open the CI pipeline log to find the rule failure details.

info.p: In GitHub Actions: navigate to the Actions tab of the repository, select the failing workflow run, expand the Validate documentation step, and read the output.
Alternatively, if reports were uploaded as artifacts, download the validation-reports artifact and open the JSON file for your changed article.

step.cmd: Note the rule name and mitigation message for each failure.

info.p: Confirm you see the same failure. If local validation passes but CI fails, check that your local rule set and the one in CI are the same version.

step.cmd: Fix the identified failures using the mitigation messages as your guide.

info.p: For guidance on common failure types, see Read the validation report.

step.cmd: Push the fix and verify the CI check passes.

cause.title: Cause: NLTK data is missing from the CI environment
cause.p: The pipeline does not include a step to download NLTK corpora, or the NLTK cache step failed.

step.cmd: Open your workflow file (.github/workflows/validate-docs.yml) and add the NLTK download step before the validation step.

info.codeblock: - name: Download NLTK language data run: python -m nltk.downloader punkt_tab averaged_perceptron_tagger_eng

step.cmd: Commit the updated workflow file and push.
step.stepresult: The CI pipeline downloads the NLTK corpora before validation runs. For a complete workflow example including the NLTK cache step, see Add markdown-validator to a GitHub Actions workflow.

cause.title: Cause: CI uses a different rule set than local validation
cause.p: CI validates with the rule set file committed to the repository. If you are running a different version of the rule set locally, the results will differ.

step.cmd: Check the CI configuration file to see which rule set it uses.

info.p: Open .github/workflows/validate-docs.yml and look for the --rules flag in the validate step.

step.cmd: Run local validation with the same rule set path.
